perturbation.original_text: Aduro Biotech, Inc., with a place of business at 740 Heinz Avenue, Berkeley, CA 94710 ("Aduro") and IREYA B.V having an address at Staalwijkstraat 16, 2313 XR Leiden, the Netherlands, represented by Andrea van Elsas, ("Consultant") agree to all terms and conditions of this Consulting Agreement ("Agreement") dated June 1, 2020, effective as of July 1, 2020 ("Effective Date").
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro Biotech, Inc., with a place of business at 740 Heinz Avenue, Berkeley, CA 94710 ("Aduro") and IREYA B.V having an address at Staalwijkstraat 16, 2313 XR Leiden, the Netherlands, represented by Andrea van Elsas, ("Independent Service Provider") agree to all terms and conditions of this Consulting Agreement ("Agreement") dated June 1, 2020, effective as of July 1, 2020 ("Effective Date").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Consultant' to 'Independent Service Provider' creates confusion about whether these terms are interchangeable throughout the document. While the rest of the contract refers to the party as "Consultant", changing the definition in the beginning creates uncertainty. This contradicts the sections where the term "Consultant" is used, specifically the section on Nonsolicitation and Representations, where the role and obligations of the 'Consultant' are clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the Effective Date and for twelve (12) months after the termination of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Consultant shall not, without Aduro's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, solicit or encourage any employee or contractor of Aduro or its affiliates to terminate employment with, or cease providing Services to, Aduro or its affiliates. In the event of a breach of this Paragraph 7 by Consultant, Aduro shall be entitled to entry of injunctive relief. Such injunctive remedy shall be nonexclusive and shall be in addition to any and all other remedies which may be available to it at law or in equity, including without limitation, the recovery of direct, indirect, incidental, consequential and/or punitive damages.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Consultant" to "Independent Service Provider" in the introductory definition section directly contradicts the subsequent use of "Consultant" in the Nonsolicitation clause (Paragraph 7). This creates ambiguity regarding who is bound by the nonsolicitation agreement, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable due to uncertainty about whether "Independent Service Provider" and "Consultant" are intended to be the same entity. The changed text introduces misaligned terminology, a direct contradiction, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: The Service Provider's sole compensation for the Services may include equity or other benefits, as decided at the discretion of Aduro, in addition to the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Consultant' to 'Service Provider' and adding the possibility of equity or other benefits introduces ambiguity about compensation. Section 2 initially stated that 'Consultant's sole compensation' would be the hourly rate. Now, by interchanging 'Consultant' with 'Service Provider' and implying that more compensation may be granted. This will contratict with the earlier section, where the compensation method was specific.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Invoices shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the compensation terms for the 'Service Provider,' stating that compensation 'may include equity or other benefits, as decided at the discretion of Aduro, in addition to the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.' This contradicts the original clause in the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2,' implying the compensation is strictly hourly at a rate of €500. The modification introduces the possibility of additional compensation (equity or other benefits), creating a direct conflict with the original intent of the contract specifying only the hourly rate as sole compensation.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: The Individual providing services shall be responsible for applicable sales tax, if any.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the phrase 'Consultant shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes' with 'The Individual providing services shall be responsible for applicable sales tax, if any' creates a contradiction concerning tax responsibilities. The original text specifically states that the Consultant is responsible for self-employment and income taxes, reinforcing their status as an independent contractor. Changing it to 'The Individual providing services' and focusing only on 'applicable sales tax' not only broadens the language but also introduces uncertainty about whether the individual is an employee or a contractor, potentially affecting Aduro's responsibilities and liabilities. This contradicts the Section 5's intention on the independent contractor
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant's relationship to Aduro shall be that of an independent contractor. Consultant shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes. Neither party shall have any authority to bind the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly assigns tax responsibility to the Consultant, reinforcing their status as an independent contractor. The changed text, by focusing solely on 'applicable sales tax' for 'The Individual providing services,' introduces uncertainty regarding their employment status, potentially conflicting with the contradicted text which clearly defines the Consultant's relationship to Aduro as an independent contractor and their responsibility for self-employment and income taxes. This misalignment of terminology regarding tax obligations and employment status creates a strong in-text contradiction, as per the definition provided.
